rulings.Case Title: CHROMADIVERSE, INC., VS. DANCE THEATRE OF HARLEM, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Apr-25-2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFF CHROMADIVERSE, INC.'s, A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings. ***PART ONE OF TWO*** On June 8, 2023 Plaintiff Chromadiverse, Inc. and Defendant Dance Theatre of Harlem entered into a joint stipulation staying the matter pending resolution of related proceedings. In short, the terms of the agreement provided the proceedings in this court would be stayed for one year from the date of filing the joint stipulation and order to (1) determine whether Marbeth's estate needed to be probated to determine the rightful heir of the photograph collection and to find the appropriate forum for the proceedings, and (2) complete the probate proceedings, or initiate the action in the proper forum. Nearly a year after the expiration of the stipulation, Chromadiverse seeks to lift the stay of proceedings because the parties entered into a new joint stipulation to lift the stay of proceedings on December 6, 2024. (Ex. B.) The parties' joint stipulation filing was rejected by the court on January 16, 2025 because a judge of this court determined a hearing following noticed motion was required. (Ex. C.) Chromadiverse contends that if the stay is not lifted, it will be prejudiced because two key witnesses in this matter are now in their mid-90s, the harms in this matter are ongoing, and further delay in this case will impede Chromadiverse's ability to obtain relief. However, specially appearing defendant Dance Theatre filed a Response arguing that the matter should remain stayed because it filed a quasi in rem action on April 10, 2025 in the Southern District of New York ("SDNY") seeking a declaration that it is the exclusive owner of all rights to the Schnare archive which includes the Marbeth photograph collection. (Ex. 4) Dance Theatre contends the matter should be stayed pending the outcome of the SDNY action because the Marbeth photograph collection is within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Copyright Act, and the photographs at issue are located in New York. Chromadiverse argues the newly filed quasi in rem proceeding is not dispositive of all the issues in this case. Generally, stay of the California proceedings is not a matter of right, but within the trial court's discretion. Here, however, the stay was effected not by a discretionary decision of this court but by an agreement of the parties that expired by its terms on June 9, 2024. Moreover, at least some of the claims at issue in this case will not be resolved by the SDNY action. Were the court deciding a stay motion in the first instance, where the burden would lie with the party seeking the stay, it would have to weigh hardships to the parties. Chromadiverse presents a factual case for hardship (the risk of unavailability of elderly witnesses), while Dance Theatre makes no such factual showing. The court therefore grants the motion and sets May 26, 2025 as Dance Theatre's deadline to respond to the complaint and/or summons. This order is without prejudice to any forum non conveniens or similar motion Dance Theatre may file. ***END OF PART ONE OF TWO*** =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Apr-25-2025, Line 4. 2-DEFENDANT JOSEPH TALMADGE , INDIVIDUALS, ARLENE TALMADGE, JOSEPH TALMADGE, BEVIN LEW, ALEXIS GARROD, CF COLLECTIVE, HOPPER LIFE LLC, TALMADGE & TALMADGE, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants move to strike portions of plaintiff Jason Cook's Third Amended Complaint (TAC). For the reasons stated herein, the motion is granted in part and denied in part. Defendants seek to strike the prayer for punitive damages in paragraph 127 as to the Seventh Cause of Action for unjust enrichment. The court grants this motion. Unjust enrichment is a quasi-contract theory of recovery. Punitive damages may not be recovered on a breach of contract; it follows that they cannot be recovered on the equitable remedy that supplies like recovery where a contract is unenforceable. ***THE FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Apr-25-2025, Line 4. 1-DEFENDANTS JOSEPH TALMADGE , INDIVIDUALS, ARLENE TALMADGE, JOSEPH TALMADGE, BEVIN LEW, ALEXIS GARROD, CF COLLECTIVE, HOPPER LIFE LLC, and TALMADGE & TALMADGE, INC.'S DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff Jason Cook's Third Amended Complaint (TAC) is overruled in part and sustained in part as set forth herein. A demurrer tests the sufficiency of the complaint on its face and "accept[s] the truth of material facts properly pleaded in the operative complaint." (Capito v. San Jose Healthcare System, LP (2024) 17 Cal.5th 273 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) Trial courts must liberally construe a pleading with a view to substantial justice between the parties, regardless of the titles and labels attached to it. (Thomas v. Regents of University of California (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 587, 605.) "If the complaint states a cause of action under any theory, regardless of the title under which the factual basis for relief is stated, that aspect of the complaint is good against a demurrer." (Id. [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) ***THE FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. This matter will be specially set at 1:30 pm and the hearing will be conducted in Department 301. Counsel and parties may access the hearing remotely via the below Zoom links to Department 301. Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the February 20, 2025 order granting judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend as to plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims is denied. Even assuming that plaintiff has shown new or different facts or circumstances that properly invoke the reconsideration procedure of CCP 1008, which is doubtful at best, the court remains of the view that the motion for judgment on the pleading was properly granted without leave to amend as to plaintiff's sex and pregnancy harassment claims. rulings.Case Title: WESTLAKE SERVICES, LLC VS. KATRINA A YIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Friday, Apr-25-2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT JEREMIAH SHAFFER'S Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents. Defendant Jeremiah Shaffer's unopposed motion to deem the truth of any matters specified in his Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Plaintiff Westlake Services, LLC failed to respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the requests for admission is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) CCP 2033.280(c) further provides that sanctions are mandatory in this circumstance, although Shaffer did not request them. The court awards sanctions of $100 payable by Plaintiff to Shaffer within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
